item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't think it needs a whole MTF dedicated to finding more of them, maybe one of the technical MTFs could scan for it on social media, or a temporary MTF could sweep foundation sites for its influence. But not a permanent task force. Downvote

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: There really isn't an argument against it, now is there? ;) Go for it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer removed. Edits implemented.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Change this:

The words 'The Factory' are found on the cardboard backing attached to each clear plastic packaging.

To this:

The words 'The Factory' are found on the cardboard backing attached to each clear plastic packaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of a blonde joke but not in a bad way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No need to be sorry, it's just one of the English language's many idiosyncracies. No harm, no foul. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's okay. There's nothing to be sorry about. If anything, we learn something new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
uh-oh, i didn't know that.
sorry…
why did i ask it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed it is. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I may not be the author of the work, but "die" is the singular form for "dice".

For reference: http://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/die#English (Under Nouns).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i'm on translating this scp to korean, and i found this

the subject will continue to roll the die indefinitely

is this an typing error? or purposed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it until the transcripts. They were too clichè and obvious. I'd have liked to see more subtle things that could be inadvertently dangerous, like…

"I don't remember if I took my medication today. Should I take a dose just in case? *rolls*"
"This is a scary-looking popup. Do I click it? *rolls*"
"This thing is overheating and security is on its way. Should I stick my hand in it and try to fix it first? *rolls*"
"Do I kill Commissioner Gordon's son *rolls*"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soo… what happens when someone rolls it without asking a question? Or what if they ask if they should do something impossible?

No one rolls them without asking a question and they ask them questions based on decisions they need to take. That's the dice's effect, as worded ad verbatim in the article.

Lot of technical errors with this one, but it was done so well I'll upvote.

Feel free to point them out so I can fix them. Thanks for your upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Soo… what happens when someone rolls it without asking a question? Or what if they ask if they should do something impossible? Lot of technical errors with this one, but it was done so well I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you're actually the first to make that comment. Thanks for the upvote though. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should I upvote?

*Roll*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess so. And then he'd stop again, having violated the dice's verdict. Not sure how that would work out for him though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If he's told not to breathe, and he holds his breath long enough that he loses consciousness, won't he start breathing again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted at cosmic sans MS because it seemed the kind of unnecessary details that would make an article
and then I wanted to upvote again at should I breathe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The second test subject reminds me of this scene:

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dPY1IvAGnAM

Ah, sweet memories.

in any case, very well-done article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing they'd keep coming up 'Maybe'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if someone asks the dice if they should stop using the dice to make decisions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Browsing SCPs, Came to this one.
It's like TwoFace's coin, but anomolously so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My goodness, yes, this. This.

They have enough laying around to test them in this way. I can't see a reason why they'd test it like this for any other reason than 'what if' though. They're easy enough to neutralize.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would be interested in seeing an experiment log where the dice were weighted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get a feeling that this is something the factory would be unlikely to mass produce. Seems more like a faulty run of an otherwise semi-functional product (where yes and no are binding, but the compulsion to roll isn't so strong).

But even though it doesn't quite fit with how I picture the factory, I still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd certainly hope not. Answer dice that make you increasingly dependent on them for every decision, even the normally unconsciously taken ones, would be the world's suckiest party favor ever. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome SCP, but isn't this what normal answer dice do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of that old-school Twilight Zone episode with Shatner, the one where he gets obsessed with the coin-op fortune-telling machine that is always right and wants to structure his life around what it says. Particularly the anonymous second couple from the end of the episode.

I like the breathing bit, it is a really good underline to how bad this effect would get. I don't think most readers would realize how many "decisions" we make all the time and how much suffering there could be if the werent' made automatically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm liking it, and I haven't been around long enough to have read another dice SCP. So, upvote from me, because I'm voting on everything today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me a lot of the SCP that makes you indecisive. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. It's inevitable that eventually, someone will pull off a new and interesting take on an SCP cliche, and this is one of them. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dice done right. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While there's a luck element involved (you roll dice, natch), this has no luck effect. That is, it's not, "pick up this die and soon you become lucky!" "Roll the dice and the number makes you do stuff!" I think I stand corrected on my previous assessment of dice SCPs.

That said, I still hope there are no more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me laugh enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. It is not a very interesting concept to me, but I must say that this is typed out very well. I also like the one bit at the end of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. :) I could have simply described the poor guy sucking in air while trying to speak, but this seemed to punch it home a lot better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn the factory are such jackasses. Also, two successful dice SCPs! Who'da thunk it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the concept (especially since it's a dice SCP that isn't COMPLETELY random in what it does)
Then those logs. The shrunken "should I breathe?" might not be in a "proper scientific report", but considering the fact that it's the transcription of what can be assumed to be the literal last bit of air this poor bastard would ever have…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, please no. A random-number generator that has such profound influence over a human's mind, to the point of overriding autonomic functions, is quite freaky enough, I would think. Making it sentient would just be over the top.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like dice SCP in general. Even with amazing writing, the concept can't be saved for me. Sorry. Still better than vast majority of dice SCP though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to steer away from the idea that the dice themselves were in some way sentient, but would that have made it more attractive to you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effect seems obvious from the dice, again, a dice SCP that's based purely on luck. The execution here has been flat out that, which doesn't make it interesting. The hook you have here has already been expected, given the dice and effect. It's not bad, but somewhat boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what, I just read through it again, and the last interview log clinched it. I must have skimmed it a bit the first time I read it, but that's really nice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TheLonging and deValmont, anything you see that could be changed to win you over?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure about this, it's quite similar to the 'Dice Man' novels, and doesn't really grab me. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the dice topic better than most, but I still don't like the concept too much. No vote, but good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An attempt at doing dice that don't have random effects. Feel free to give me your comments and tell me if there's anything that could use editing, rephrasing or deleting.
